text:
There once was a hummingbird who lived in a pink garden. Every day after finishing her breakfast of ants, she zipped through the blossom-filled air, feeding on nectar, gathering pollen for flowers, and chasing away mosquitoes. She was known for her marvelous flight and graceful dances.

One sunny afternoon, as she buzzed around searching for flowers, she noticed something different. A patch of dandelions had grown overnight, big and fluffy. She chuckled to herself, enjoying a challenge. Off she went, buzzing past the dandelions with surprising precision. Each time she landed on a bud, she'd poke it gently with her tiny bill.

To her delight, the dandelions started wilting. She enjoyed watching them bloom anew as she continued her pollination quest.

Soon, it was time for her nightly vigil. She fluttered into a lantern-shaped flower that nightted softly. Asleep, she dreamt of flowers chatting about how they could defeat the invaders—mites—using nothing
